quote_text: We do not have a very short time assigned to us, but we lose a great deal of it.
attribution: Seneca, On the Shortness of Life I
reflection: Where are you leaking time that could be returned to purpose?

quote_text: If it be abusive words, you will find it to be patience.
reflection: What insult or irritation can become practice in patience?

quote_text: In all places, and at all times, it is in thy power religiously to embrace whatsoever by God's appointment is happened unto thee.
reflection: What fact of today can you meet without resistance?

quote_text: Grant them to come to that Wisdom Wherewithal, sistered with Justice, Thou rulest and governest all things.
reflection: How can wisdom and justice stay joined in your next decision?

quote_text: It is in my power concerning this thing that is happened, what soever it be, to conceit that which is right and true.
reflection: What is the truest way to describe the situation in front of you?

quote_text: The highest good is a mind which despises the accidents of fortune, and takes pleasure in virtue.
attribution: Seneca, On the Happy Life IV
reflection: What would you do if virtue mattered more than outcome today?

quote_text: Whatsoever any man either doth or saith, thou must be good.
reflection: Who might test your character, and how will you remain good?

quote_text: Never say about anything, I have lost it, but say I have restored it.
reflection: What loss can you hold more lightly by remembering it was never owned?

quote_text: We do not receive a short life, but we make it a short one.
attribution: Seneca, On the Shortness of Life I
reflection: What habit is making your life feel smaller than it is?

quote_text: The understanding is of itself sufficient unto itself.
reflection: Where are you asking for reassurance that your reason can provide?

quote_text: Despise fortune; I have given her no weapon that can reach the mind.
reflection: What external reversal cannot touch your power to choose well?

quote_text: Wipe off all opinion stay the force and violence of unreasonable lusts and affections.
reflection: What opinion or impulse should you wipe away before acting?

quote_text: Whatsoever doth happen in the world, doth happen justly, and so if thou dost well take heed, thou shalt find it.
reflection: What would change if you looked for order before complaint?

quote_text: Readiness to believe what we hear causes very great mischief.
reflection: What rumor or assumption should you refuse to spread today?

quote_text: Seek not that the things which happen should happen as you wish; but wish the things which happen to be as they are.
reflection: What reality would cost less energy if you stopped arguing with it?

quote_text: On the occasion of every accident (event) that befalls you, remember to turn to yourself and inquire what power you have for turning it to use.
reflection: What accident can you turn into useful practice?

quote_text: Chiefest glory of deathless Gods, Almighty for ever, Sovereign of Nature that rulest by law.
reflection: What law or principle should rule your choices today?

quote_text: Virtue will never be guilty of imitating vice while she is repressing it.
reflection: How can you oppose wrong without copying its spirit?

quote_text: If you would improve, submit to be considered without sense and foolish with respect to externals.
reflection: What reputation cost are you willing to pay for real progress?

quote_text: A man's true happiness doth consist in very few things.
reflection: What can be removed from your idea of happiness today?

quote_text: Life is long enough to carry out the most important projects.
attribution: Seneca, On the Shortness of Life I
reflection: What important work deserves a protected hour today?

quote_text: Hold on to the things which seem to you best as one appointed by God to this station.
reflection: What post have you been given that asks for resolve?

quote_text: Among those whom you see in the garb of peace there is no peace.
reflection: How can seeing human imperfection clearly make you more patient?

quote_text: Wish to be considered to know nothing.
reflection: Where would humility free you from performing certainty?

quote_text: Word after word, every one by itself, must the things that are spoken be conceived and understood.
reflection: What conversation needs slower and more exact listening?

quote_text: Anger can be put to flight by wise maxims.
reflection: What principle will you rehearse before irritation arrives?

quote_text: He felt more confidence in his friend: he deserved that his friend should be innocent.
reflection: Where can trust be the more just first assumption?

quote_text: You ought to be somebody in those things only which are in your power.
reflection: Where can you define success by character rather than status?

quote_text: We ought to fight against the first causes of evil.
reflection: What small cause should you address before it becomes a larger fault?

quote_text: Whensoever any man doth trespass against other, presently consider with thyself what it was that he did suppose to be good.
reflection: How can understanding another person's mistaken good reduce your anger?

quote_text: Be satisfied then in everything with being a philosopher.
reflection: What is enough today if your aim is to live wisely?
